name: AFFERENT LOOP SYNDROME
definition: PROXIMAL LOOP / BLIND LOOP SYNDROME partial intermittent obstruction of afferent loop leading to overdistension of loop by gastric
dominant_finding: oral contrast material may not enter loop
discriminating_features: normal in 85% (no air in lumen of afferent loop)
preferential emptying of stomach into proximal loop
rounded water-density masses adjacent to head + tail of pancreas forming a U-shaped loop

general: normal in 85% (no air in lumen of afferent loop)
preferential emptying of stomach into proximal loop
CT: rounded water-density masses adjacent to head + tail of pancreas forming a U-shaped loop
oral contrast material may not enter loop
may result in biliary obstruction (increased pressure at ampulla)
